title: Gawd Voice Configuration
description: Voice modality config block. Source of truth lives in IDENTITY.md (T0 anchor) under the 'Voice' section; this schema validates that block plus the supplementary runtime fields read from ~/.gawd/state/voice.json. Per Architecture §9: voice is model-agnostic — STT/LLM/TTS providers are all swappable. Per gospel §1 principle 1: never hardwire a provider or model name into code.

enabled.description: When false, voice subsystem fails graceful and the daemon runs text-only. Per acceptance criterion 8, voice is opt-in; missing keys must not break daemon startup.

voice_id.description: Per-Gawd-per-Prophit TTS voice identifier. Provider-specific (e.g., ElevenLabs voice ID 'EXAVITQu4vr4xnSDxMaL'). Lives in IDENTITY.md per spec §9.4. The Gawd does NOT change this mid-relationship without Prophit consent; changes are logged in MEMORY.md and routed through the /voice slash command (see runbook §5).

llm.description: LLM section for the voice loop. Reads through the same chain as the rest of the daemon (via gawdfather-llm-call), so model swaps in OpenClaw config apply here too. NEVER hardwire model names in voice code.

mode.description: How the voice loop resolves the model. 'chain' = use the OpenClaw primary chain (default; same model the Gawd uses elsewhere). 'tier' = use a Pantheon tier hint (spec §6.5) — e.g., blessed for fast/cheap voice replies. 'explicit' = use a specific model id (escape hatch; flagged as drift in audit logs).

tier.description: Required when mode='tier'. Selects which Pantheon rung handles voice-loop LLM calls. Lower tiers are appropriate for voice because turns are short and latency-sensitive.

model_id.description: Required when mode='explicit'. Provider/model string per the OpenClaw chain convention (format: '<provider>/<model-id>'). Only used when the operator explicitly chose to pin; logged as drift in the daily audit. NOTE: pinning a specific model here recreates the hardwire pattern this subsystem is designed to eliminate.

max_tokens.description: Cap on response length. Voice replies should be short by default; long replies break the cadence of a spoken conversation.

stt.description: Speech-to-text provider config. ElevenLabs is the default but the field is provider-agnostic; any provider implementing the transcribe(audio_bytes) interface qualifies. See runbook §3 for adding alternate providers.

provider.description: Provider implementation to load at startup. 'mock' is for tests (deterministic transcription output). 'local-whisper' is the privacy-preserving option but requires the local whisper.cpp server (port not yet specified in spec).

api_key_env.description: Name of the environment variable that holds the API key. NEVER the key value itself. The secrets helper (~/.local/bin/secrets) writes the env var at runtime.

language.description: STT language hint. 'auto' lets the provider detect. Otherwise an ISO 639-1 code (e.g., 'en', 'es'). Default 'auto' covers multi-language households.

endpoint_override.description: Optional: replace the provider's default API endpoint. Used for self-hosted or proxied deployments. Documented in runbook §4.

tts.description: Text-to-speech provider config. Mirrors STT structure. ElevenLabs default; voice_id at the top level is what gets passed to the provider's speak() call.

provider.description: Provider implementation. 'mock' for tests. 'local-piper' is the local privacy-preserving option.

api_key_env.description: Env var name (not the value). Often the same as STT for ElevenLabs.

format.description: Output audio format. 'ogg_opus' is preferred for Telegram voice notes (native format). 'pcm_16000' for browser-relay raw audio streaming.

stability.description: Provider-specific tuning knob; harmless to non-ElevenLabs providers (ignored).

browser_relay.description: Browser-mic WebSocket relay config. Required only if the relay is wired up; the daemon runs voice-via-Telegram fine without it.

enabled.description: When false, the browser-relay systemd unit stays inactive.

port.description: WebSocket server port. Loopback-bound; exposed externally via cloudflared (Prophit-VM/bare-metal) or Tailscale Serve (hosted rung).

bind_address.description: Bind address. Default loopback. Operators who choose to bind 0.0.0.0 must front the relay with a TLS terminator and auth — never expose raw.

max_session_seconds.description: Cap on a single relay session. Defense against runaway loops and stuck mic-open conditions.

cloudflared_hostname.description: FQDN the cloudflared tunnel exposes (e.g., 'lilith.gawd.sh'). Single-level subdomain only (the wildcard cert at gawd.sh does not cover multi-level).

allowed_origins.description: BLOCKER 4: Origin allowlist for the WebSocket upgrade (e.g., ['https://lilith.gawd.sh']). If empty and cloudflared_hostname is set, the relay derives ['https://<cloudflared_hostname>']. If both are empty, ALL browser origins are rejected (fail closed) — an explicit opt-in is required to expose the relay.

telegram_voice.description: Telegram voice-note handling. v1 commitment per acceptance criterion 7 — ships day-1 whether browser-relay does or does not.

enabled.description: Default ON. Telegram is the primary surface; voice-note handling is free given the bot already exists.

auto_reply_format.description: How the Gawd replies to a voice-note. 'voice' = TTS-generated voice-note back. 'text+voice' = both (Prophit gets transcript + audio). 'text' = text only (useful if TTS provider is unavailable).

max_voice_seconds.description: Cap on inbound voice-note length. Longer notes get a polite refusal asking for a transcript or breakdown.

fail_graceful.description: What the daemon does when voice infrastructure breaks. Per gospel §1 principle reliability + handoff acceptance criterion 8: voice is optional; failures must not cascade.

on_stt_failure.description: If STT fails to transcribe: 'text_apology' = reply in text explaining; 'silent_drop' = log and ignore; 'retry_once_then_text' = one retry then fall back to text.

on_tts_failure.description: If TTS fails to synthesize: degrade to text reply. Never silently drop the Gawd's response.

on_missing_api_key.description: At daemon startup, if voice is enabled but STT/TTS keys missing. Default 'disable_voice' means daemon comes up text-only and voice features unbind. Per acceptance criterion 8: voice must NEVER block daemon startup.
